evaluation.summary: The scaling exponent for VMR in primorial gaps deviates from the previously assumed 0.80 for k≥8, yielding a fitted exponent of 0.8435 with lower R², suggesting the earlier model is not robust.
next_directions: Extend the VMR analysis to larger primorial indices (k>10) to assess asymptotic behavior.
Conduct a rigorous error analysis to rule out boundary effects, truncation, and computational artifacts.
Develop a theoretical framework linking VMR scaling to underlying number-theoretic properties of primorial gaps.
